key_points: All matter is made up of extremely small particles
Particles of matter have spaces between them
Particles of matter attract each other
Particles of matter are in constant motion
The three main states of matter are solids, liquids, and gases
examples: Grinding chalk into powder shows matter is made of tiny particles
Sugar dissolving in water shows spaces between particles
Perfume smell spreading in a room demonstrates diffusion
Air in a syringe can be compressed showing large spaces between gas particles
Potassium permanganate spreading in water shows particle movement
formulas_or_processes: Diffusion: Movement of particles from a region of higher concentration to lower concentration
Boiling: Liquid → Gas at boiling temperature
Evaporation: Liquid → Gas below boiling point from the surface
exam_tips: Remember the main characteristics of solids, liquids, and gases
Understand experiments demonstrating particulate nature of matter
Revise concepts such as diffusion, melting, boiling, and evaporation
Use particle diagrams to explain the properties of different states of matter
